content.text:
I can create the GroupInterface NodeType as specified, but I ran into the mesh context issue: the Cornerstone address/collections (e.g., @Cornerstone/Insured and @Cornerstone/Pricing) aren’t mapped/available in the mesh right now. Before I proceed, please confirm one of the options below:

1) Proceed and create the NodeType at @Cornerstone/GroupInterface plus minimal placeholder schemas for Cornerstone/Insured and Cornerstone/Pricing (so reference paths resolve). I will follow the conventions used by existing Cornerstone files (create simple Insured and Pricing content type schemas with key fields used by GroupInterface). I will mark the NodeType reusable and include the layout areas you specified. (If the icon path is invalid I’ll still record it and you can update later.)

2) You’ll provide the exact Cornerstone mapping/context or existing Insured.json and Pricing.json to mirror; I will then create GroupInterface to match those schemas precisely.

3) Stop — don’t create anything yet; provide further edits or clarifications (e.g., field constraints, indexing, any additional fields, preferred DateTime format, specific layout widget types).

If you choose option 1, I will:
- Create NodeType at @Cornerstone/GroupInterface with the NodeTypeDefinition you gave:
  - WithContentType<GroupInterface>()
  - Content model fields: Id (string key), InsuredId (reference to @Cornerstone/Insured), InsuredName (string), PricingIds (string[] referencing @Cornerstone/Pricing), AggregatedTSI (decimal), TotalLocations (int), PrimaryCountry (string), LineOfBusiness (string), Status (string), Notes (markdown), LastUpdated (DateTime).
  - AddData left empty (instances can be created under insured namespaces).
  - AddDefaultLayoutAreas() plus the Layout areas Overview, InsuredSummary, PricingSummary, AggregatedReport, Mappings with the details you provided.
  - Mark reusable across Cornerstone insureds and ensure reference paths are @Cornerstone/Insured and @Cornerstone/Pricing.
- Create minimal placeholder Cornerstone/Insured and Cornerstone/Pricing content type schemas (only required fields referenced by GroupInterface) so references resolve.

Please confirm which option (1, 2, or 3) you prefer and whether I should proceed with creating the placeholder Insured/Pricing schemas and the NodeType now.
